Tests for the workout service in the Pebble firmware, including scenarios for starting, pausing, resuming, and stopping workouts, as well as handling heart rate data and workout abandonment.

# Purpose
The code is a test suite for a workout service module. It includes various test functions to verify the functionality of a workout service, which manages workout sessions, heart rate monitoring, and activity tracking. The code is organized into several test cases, each focusing on different aspects of the workout service, such as starting and stopping workouts, handling heart rate data, managing workout sessions, and ensuring correct behavior when the application is opened or closed.

The test suite uses a series of stub functions and fake components to simulate the environment in which the workout service operates. These include stubs for activity sessions, heart rate monitoring, and time management. The test cases cover scenarios such as pausing and resuming workouts, handling heart rate zone calculations, and managing workout sessions across different time periods, including past midnight. The tests also ensure that the workout service correctly handles edge cases, such as invalid workouts and abandoned sessions. The suite uses assertions to verify that the workout service behaves as expected under various conditions.

- `services/normal/activity/activity.h`

# Global Variables

---
### s\_saved\_session
- **Type**: ``ActivitySession``
- **Description**: Stores a copy of the most recent `ActivitySession` that is added through the `activity_sessions_prv_add_activity_session` function. This variable is static, meaning it is only accessible within the file it is declared in.
- **Use**: Used to retain the state of the last added activity session for later retrieval or processing.


---
### s\_hrm\_subscribed
- **Type**: ``bool``
- **Description**: Indicates if the heart rate monitor (HRM) is currently subscribed or active. This variable is used to track the subscription status of the HRM service.
- **Use**: Used to determine if the HRM service is active or needs to be activated or deactivated.


---
### s\_hrm\_expiration
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Stores the expiration time in seconds for a heart rate monitor (HRM) session. This variable is used to determine when the HRM session should expire.
- **Use**: Used to set and check the expiration time for HRM sessions in the `sys_hrm_manager_app_subscribe` and `sys_hrm_manager_unsubscribe` functions.


---
### s\_total\_step\_count
- **Type**: ``uint32_t``
- **Description**: Stores the total number of steps counted during a workout session. It is a static variable, meaning it is only accessible within the file it is declared in.
- **Use**: Used to keep track of the cumulative step count and update it during workout sessions.


---
### s\_abandoned\_workout\_notification\_sent
- **Type**: ``bool``
- **Description**: Indicates if the notification for an abandoned workout has been sent. This variable is used to track the state of the notification process for workouts that are not completed.
- **Use**: Used to prevent sending multiple notifications for the same abandoned workout.

### sys\_hrm\_manager\_app\_subscribe<!-- {{#callable:sys_hrm_manager_app_subscribe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L99>)

Subscribes an application to the heart rate monitor (HRM) service with specified parameters.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``app_id``: The identifier for the application that wants to subscribe to the HRM service.
    - ``update_interval_s``: The interval in seconds at which the HRM updates should occur.
    - ``expire_s``: The time in seconds after which the subscription will expire.
    - ``features``: The features of the HRM that the application wants to use.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the global variable `s_hrm_subscribed` to `true`, indicating that the HRM service is now subscribed.
    - Sets the global variable `s_hrm_expiration` to the value of `expire_s`, indicating the expiration time for the subscription.
    - Returns a constant value `1`, which likely represents a successful subscription.
- **Output**: Returns a constant value `1`, which is likely a placeholder for a valid `HRMSessionRef`.


---
### sys\_hrm\_manager\_unsubscribe<!-- {{#callable:sys_hrm_manager_unsubscribe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L106>)

Unsubscribes from the heart rate monitor session by setting the subscription status to false and expiration to zero.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ref``: A reference to the heart rate monitor session (`HRMSessionRef`) that is to be unsubscribed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Set the global variable `s_hrm_subscribed` to `false` to indicate that the heart rate monitor is no longer subscribed.
    - Set the global variable `s_hrm_expiration` to `0` to reset the expiration time for the heart rate monitor session.
    - Return `true` to indicate successful unsubscription.
- **Output**: Returns `true` to indicate that the unsubscription was successful.

### time\_get\_uptime\_seconds<!-- {{#callable:time_get_uptime_seconds}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L112>)

Calculates the system uptime in seconds by adding a constant number of seconds per day to the current time from the RTC.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `rtc_get_time()` to get the current time from the RTC.
    - Adds `SECONDS_PER_DAY` to the result of `rtc_get_time()`.
    - Returns the sum as the uptime in seconds.
- **Output**: Returns a `uint32_t` representing the system uptime in seconds.


---
### activity\_get\_metric<!-- {{#callable:activity_get_metric}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L117>)

Checks if the `metric` is `ActivityMetricStepCount` and assigns `s_total_step_count` to `history` if true.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``metric``: An `ActivityMetric` value that specifies the type of activity metric to retrieve.
    - ``history_len``: A `uint32_t` value representing the length of the history array, though it is not used in the function.
    - ``history``: A pointer to an `int32_t` where the function will store the total step count if the metric is `ActivityMetricStepCount`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `metric` is equal to `ActivityMetricStepCount`.
    - If `metric` is not `ActivityMetricStepCount`, return `false`.
    - If `metric` is `ActivityMetricStepCount`, assign `s_total_step_count` to the location pointed by `history`.
    - Return `true` if `metric` is `ActivityMetricStepCount`.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if `metric` is `ActivityMetricStepCount` and assigns `s_total_step_count` to `history`; otherwise, returns `false`.

### prv\_inc\_steps\_and\_put\_event<!-- {{#callable:prv_inc_steps_and_put_event}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L131>)

Increments the total step count by a given number of steps and triggers a health event update.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``steps``: The number of steps to add to the total step count.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Add the input `steps` to the global variable `s_total_step_count`.
    - Create a `PebbleEvent` structure with type `PEBBLE_HEALTH_SERVICE_EVENT` and a nested `health_event` of type `HealthEventMovementUpdate`.
    - Set the `steps` field of the `movement_update` data in the event to the updated `s_total_step_count`.
    - Call `event_put` to dispatch the event.
    - Invoke `workout_service_health_event_handler` with the `health_event` from the event.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates through side effects on global state and event dispatching.


---
### prv\_put\_bpm\_event<!-- {{#callable:prv_put_bpm_event}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L147>)

Creates a `PebbleEvent` for a heart rate update and sends it to the event system and workout service handler.
- **Inputs**:
    - `bpm`: The current beats per minute (BPM) value to include in the event.
    - `quality`: The quality of the heart rate measurement, represented by the `HRMQuality` enum.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initializes a `PebbleEvent` structure with type `PEBBLE_HEALTH_SERVICE_EVENT` and a nested `health_event` of type `HealthEventHeartRateUpdate`.
    - Sets the `current_bpm` and `quality` fields of the `heart_rate_update` data structure within the event to the provided `bpm` and `quality` values.
    - Calls `event_put` to send the constructed event to the event system.
    - Invokes `workout_service_health_event_handler` with the `health_event` part of the event to handle the heart rate update in the workout service.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates by side effects, sending events to the system.


---

### test\_workout\_service\_\_initialize<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__initialize}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L167>)

Initializes the test workout service by setting up the fake RTC, resetting the workout service, initializing step count and other variables, and initializing the workout service.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`fake_rtc_init`](<../../../fakes/fake_rtc.c.md#fake_rtc_init>) with parameters `0, 0` to initialize the fake RTC.
    - Calls [`workout_service_reset`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_reset>) to reset the workout service state.
    - Sets `s_total_step_count` to `5000` to initialize the step count for the day.
    - Sets `s_hrm_expiration` to `0` and `s_hrm_subscribed` to `false` to initialize heart rate monitor variables.
    - Sets `s_abandoned_workout_notification_sent` to `false` to initialize the workout notification state.
    - Calls [`fake_mutex_reset`](<../../../fakes/fake_mutex.h.md#fake_mutex_reset>) with `assert_all_unlocked` set to `true` to reset the fake mutex.
    - Calls `workout_service_init` to initialize the workout service.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`fake_rtc_init`](<../../../fakes/fake_rtc.c.md#fake_rtc_init>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L193>)

Tests the basic functionality of the workout service by simulating a workout session and verifying the initial and updated workout metrics.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Starts a workout session with `workout_service_start_workout` and asserts its success.
    - Initializes variables for steps, duration, distance, bpm, and HR zone.
    - Retrieves and asserts initial workout metrics using `workout_service_get_current_workout_info`.
    - Simulates time and step increments, updates metrics, and asserts expected values.
    - Simulates heart rate events, updates metrics, and asserts expected values.
    - Repeats the process of simulating time, steps, and heart rate events, updating metrics, and asserting expected values.
    - Stops the workout session with `workout_service_stop_workout` and asserts its success.
    - Verifies that no workout information is available after stopping the workout.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function uses assertions to validate the workout service's behavior.

[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L249>)

Tests the takeover of an activity session by the workout service and verifies the session data and calculations.
- **Inputs**:
    - `void`: No input parameters.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize variables for steps, duration, distance, bpm, and active calories.
    - Increment time by 610 seconds to simulate session start.
    - Create an `ActivitySession` with specific parameters and start it.
    - Assert that the workout service takes over the session successfully.
    - Retrieve and assert current workout information for steps, duration, and distance.
    - Retrieve and assert active calories match the session's initial value.
    - Increment time by 600 seconds and add steps to simulate ongoing activity.
    - Retrieve new workout information and calculate active calories based on distance change.
    - Assert that the new active calories have increased compared to the initial value.
    - Verify calculated active calories match the workout service's reported increase.
    - Stop the workout and verify the stored session data matches expected values.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:

### test\_workout\_service\_\_pause\_resume<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__pause_resume}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L322>)

Tests the pause and resume functionality of the workout service by simulating workout data and verifying the correct behavior of workout statistics during paused and resumed states.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize variables for steps, duration, distance, bpm, and hr_zone.
    - Start a workout session of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and increment time and steps, then simulate a heart rate event with bpm 100.
    - Assert that the workout information matches the expected values for steps, duration, and bpm.
    - Pause the workout and simulate additional time, steps, and heart rate events, asserting that only the heart rate changes while other stats remain unchanged.
    - Resume the workout, simulate more data, and assert that all stats update correctly.
    - Pause the workout twice consecutively and assert that stats remain unchanged except for heart rate.
    - Simulate more data while paused and assert that only heart rate changes.
    - Resume the workout, simulate more data, and assert that all stats update correctly.
    - Stop the workout session.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this function is a test case that uses assertions to verify behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)
    - [`prv_inc_steps_and_put_event`](<#prv_inc_steps_and_put_event>)
    - [`prv_put_bpm_event`](<#prv_put_bpm_event>)

Tests the expiration of heart rate readings during a workout session.
- **Inputs**:
    - `void`: No input parameters.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize variables for steps, duration, distance, bpm, and hr_zone.
    - Start a workout session of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and assert its success.
    - Simulate a heart rate event with 100 bpm and good quality.
    - Retrieve current workout information and assert that bpm is 100.
    - Increment time by 30 seconds and assert that bpm remains 100 after retrieving workout information again.
    - Increment time by another 30 seconds and assert that bpm is now 0, indicating the heart rate reading has expired.
    - Stop the workout session and assert its success.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs assertions to validate behavior.

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize variables for steps, duration, distance, bpm, and hr_zone.
    - Assert that a workout session starts with `workout_service_start_workout`.

    - Send an off-wrist heart rate reading of 50 bpm and verify it is received by checking that bpm is set to 0 and hr_zone is set to `HRZone_Zone0`.
    - Send another good quality heart rate reading of 100 bpm and verify it is accepted by checking the bpm value.

### test\_workout\_service\_\_working\_out\_past\_midnight<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__working_out_past_midnight}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L464>)

Tests the behavior of the workout service when a workout session continues past midnight, ensuring step counts are correctly updated and maintained.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Start a workout session of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and assert it starts successfully.
    - Increment steps by 10 and assert the step count updates to 10.
    - Increment steps by 1000 and assert the step count updates to 1010.
    - Simulate midnight by resetting `s_total_step_count` to 0.
    - Increment steps by 50 and assert the step count updates to 1060, ensuring no data loss or errors occur.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this function is a test case that uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_steps_and_put_event`](<#prv_inc_steps_and_put_event>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L494>)

Tests the behavior of the workout service when the app is opened and closed without starting a workout.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increments the time by one minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>).
    - Calls `workout_service_frontend_opened` to simulate opening the app and checks that `s_hrm_subscribed` is `true` and `s_hrm_expiration` is `0`.
    - Calls `workout_service_frontend_closed` to simulate closing the app and checks that `s_hrm_subscribed` is `false` and `s_hrm_expiration` is `0`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a void function used for testing.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_app\_open\_close\_active\_workout<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__app_open_close_active_workout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L511>)

Tests the behavior of the workout service when the app is opened, a workout is started, and then the app is closed, ensuring the heart rate monitor remains active for a specified duration.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increments the time by one minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>).
    - Calls `workout_service_frontend_opened` to simulate opening the app and checks that the heart rate monitor (`s_hrm_subscribed`) is subscribed with no expiration (`s_hrm_expiration` is 0).
    - Starts a workout of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and asserts that it starts successfully.
    - Calls `workout_service_frontend_closed` to simulate closing the app and checks that the heart rate monitor remains subscribed (`s_hrm_subscribed` is true) with an expiration set to `SECONDS_PER_HOUR`.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a void function used for testing purposes.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_app\_open\_close\_ended\_invalid\_workout<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__app_open_close_ended_invalid_workout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L530>)

Tests the behavior of the workout service when an app is opened, a short workout is started and stopped, and the app is closed, ensuring the heart rate monitor is unsubscribed after an invalid workout.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increment the time by one minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>).
    - Open the workout service frontend with `workout_service_frontend_opened` and assert that the heart rate monitor (`s_hrm_subscribed`) is subscribed and expiration (`s_hrm_expiration`) is zero.
    - Increment the time by another minute and start a workout of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` using `workout_service_start_workout`, asserting the start was successful.
    - Increment the time by 30 seconds to simulate a short workout duration.
    - Stop the workout using `workout_service_stop_workout` and assert the stop was successful.
    - Close the workout service frontend with `workout_service_frontend_closed` and assert that the heart rate monitor is unsubscribed and expiration is zero.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a void function designed to perform assertions for testing purposes.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_app\_open\_close\_valid\_workout<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__app_open_close_valid_workout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L553>)

Tests the behavior of the workout service when the app is opened, a valid workout is started and stopped, and then the app is closed.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increment the time by 1 minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>).
    - Open the app with `workout_service_frontend_opened` and assert that the heart rate monitor (`s_hrm_subscribed`) is subscribed with no expiration (`s_hrm_expiration` is 0).
    - Increment the time by another minute and start a workout of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` using `workout_service_start_workout`, asserting that it starts successfully.
    - Increment the time by 2 minutes to simulate a workout duration of 120 seconds, then stop the workout using `workout_service_stop_workout`, asserting that it stops successfully.
    - Increment the time by another 2 minutes.
    - Close the app with `workout_service_frontend_closed` and assert that the heart rate monitor remains subscribed (`s_hrm_subscribed` is true) with an expiration set to 8 minutes (`s_hrm_expiration` is 8 * SECONDS_PER_MINUTE).
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a void function used for testing purposes.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_app\_open\_wait\_close\_valid\_workout<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__app_open_wait_close_valid_workout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L578>)

Simulates opening an app, starting a workout, waiting, and closing the app to verify the heart rate monitor subscription status and expiration.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increment the time by 1 minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>).
    - Open the app with `workout_service_frontend_opened` and assert that `s_hrm_subscribed` is true and `s_hrm_expiration` is 0.
    - Increment the time by another minute and start a workout with `workout_service_start_workout` for a duration of 120 seconds.
    - Increment the time by 2 minutes and stop the workout with `workout_service_stop_workout`.
    - Increment the time by 20 minutes.
    - Close the app with `workout_service_frontend_closed` and assert that `s_hrm_subscribed` is false and `s_hrm_expiration` is 0.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this is a void function used for testing.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_heart\_rate\_zone\_time<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__heart_rate_zone_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L602>)

Tests the accumulation of time spent in different heart rate zones during a workout session.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize heart rate zones and an array `hr_zone_time_s` to store time spent in each zone.
    - Start a workout session and verify initial heart rate zone times are zero.
    - Simulate heart rate events in different zones and increment time, checking that the time spent in each zone is correctly updated.
    - Pause the workout and verify that time does not accumulate in any zone during the pause.
    - Unpause the workout and continue to simulate heart rate events, verifying that time accumulation resumes.
    - Check that the function handles null pointers correctly by asserting false when [`workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time>) is called with a null argument.
    - Stop the workout and verify that no time is accumulated after stopping.
- **Output**: No direct output; the function uses assertions to verify correct behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time>)
    - [`prv_put_bpm_event`](<#prv_put_bpm_event>)
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_avg\_hr<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__avg_hr}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L701>)

Tests the average heart rate calculation during a workout session, including pause and resume scenarios.
- **Inputs**:
    - `None`: This function does not take any input parameters.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Declare an integer `avg_hr` to store the average heart rate.
    - Assert that [`workout_service_get_avg_hr`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_avg_hr>) returns false before starting a workout.
    - Start a workout session of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and assert success.
    - Simulate two heart rate events with a BPM of 140 and assert the average heart rate is 140.
    - Simulate two more heart rate events with a BPM of 160 and assert the average heart rate is 150.
    - Pause the workout and simulate two heart rate events with a BPM of 200, then assert the average heart rate remains 150.
    - Resume the workout, simulate two heart rate events with a BPM of 180, and assert the average heart rate is 160.
- **Output**: No output is returned, but assertions are used to validate the average heart rate calculations.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`workout_service_get_avg_hr`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_avg_hr>)
    - [`prv_put_bpm_event`](<#prv_put_bpm_event>)

### test\_workout\_service\_\_abandon\_workout<!-- {{#callable:test_workout_service__abandon_workout}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L736>)

Simulates the abandonment of a workout session by incrementing time, checking notifications, and ensuring the workout ends after a specified period.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Increment the time by 1 minute using [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>) to simulate time passing.
    - Open the workout app with `workout_service_frontend_opened` and increment time by another minute.
    - Start a workout session of type `ActivitySessionType_Run` and increment time by 5 minutes to simulate workout duration.
    - Close the workout app with `workout_service_frontend_closed` and increment time by 30 minutes.
    - Assert that the abandoned workout notification has not been sent and the workout is still ongoing using `cl_assert_equal_b`.
    - Increment time by 25 minutes, call [`prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback>), and assert that the notification is sent.
    - Increment time by 5 more minutes, call [`prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback>), and assert that the workout is no longer ongoing.
- **Output**: No output is returned as this function is a test case that uses assertions to verify behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_inc_time`](<#prv_inc_time>)
    - [`prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback>)
    - [`prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback>)

### prv\_abandon\_workout\_timer\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L37>)

Stops the workout service.
- **Description**: Use this function to stop the workout service when a workout session is abandoned. It is typically called as a callback function in a timer event to ensure that the workout service is stopped after a certain period of inactivity or abandonment. This function should be used in scenarios where the workout session needs to be terminated without user intervention.
- **Inputs**:
    - `unused`: This parameter is not used in the function and can be set to any value. It is typically used to match the signature required for a callback function.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandon_workout_timer_callback>)  (Implementation)

### prv\_abandoned\_notification\_timer\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L38>)

Sends an abandoned workout notification and resets the workout timer.
- **Description**: Use this function to handle the event when a workout is considered abandoned. It sends a notification about the abandoned workout and resets the workout timer to trigger another callback after a specified timeout. This function is typically used in scenarios where a workout session needs to be monitored for inactivity and appropriate actions are taken when the session is deemed abandoned.
- **Inputs**:
    - `unused`: This parameter is not used in the function and can be set to `NULL` or any value. It is a placeholder for compatibility with callback function signatures.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_abandoned_notification_timer_callback>)  (Implementation)


---
### prv\_workout\_timer\_cb<!-- {{#callable_declaration:prv_workout_timer_cb}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L39>)

Updates workout duration and checks heart rate validity.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the workout duration and verify the validity of the heart rate data during an ongoing workout. It must be called only when a workout is in progress. If the heart rate data is outdated, it resets the heart rate information. This function does not take any input parameters and does not return any value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `unused`: This parameter is not used and can be set to NULL. It is a placeholder for compatibility with callback function signatures.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`prv_workout_timer_cb`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#prv_workout_timer_cb>)  (Implementation)

### workout\_service\_get\_avg\_hr<!-- {{#callable_declaration:workout_service_get_avg_hr}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L40>)

Retrieves the average heart rate during an ongoing workout.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the average heart rate when a workout is in progress. It requires that a workout is currently ongoing; otherwise, it will not provide a valid result. Ensure that the pointer provided is not null, as the function will return false if the pointer is null or if no workout is active. This function is useful for monitoring heart rate data during exercise sessions.
- **Inputs**:
    - `avg_hr_out`: A pointer to an `int32_t` where the function will store the average heart rate. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the memory. If the pointer is null or no workout is ongoing, the function returns false.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the average heart rate was successfully retrieved and stored in `avg_hr_out`; otherwise, returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`workout_service_get_avg_hr`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_avg_hr>)  (Implementation)


---
### workout\_service\_get\_current\_workout\_hr\_zone\_time<!-- {{#callable_declaration:workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L41>)

Retrieves the current workout heart rate zone times.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the time spent in each heart rate zone during the current workout. It must be called only when a workout is ongoing. The function will not execute if the workout is not active or if the output parameter is null. Ensure that the workout is started before calling this function to get valid data.
- **Inputs**:
    - `hr_zone_time_s_out`: A pointer to an array of `int32_t` where the function will store the time spent in each heart rate zone. The array must have space for all heart rate zones. Must not be null. If null, the function returns false and does not modify any data.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the function successfully retrieves the heart rate zone times, otherwise returns `false` if the workout is not ongoing or if the output parameter is null.
- **See Also**: [`workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_current_workout_hr_zone_time>)  (Implementation)


---
### workout\_service\_get\_active\_kcalories<!-- {{#callable_declaration:workout_service_get_active_kcalories}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L42>)

Retrieves the active kilocalories burned during an ongoing workout.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the number of active kilocalories burned during a workout session. It must be called only when a workout is ongoing, as it will not modify the output parameter if no workout is active. This function is useful for tracking calorie expenditure in real-time during a workout.
- **Inputs**:
    - `active`: A pointer to an `int32_t` where the function will store the active kilocalories. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the memory. If no workout is ongoing, the value pointed to by `active` remains unchanged.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`workout_service_get_active_kcalories`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_get_active_kcalories>)  (Implementation)

### workout\_service\_reset<!-- {{#callable_declaration:workout_service_reset}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/test_workout_service.c#L43>)

Resets the workout service state.
- **Description**: Use this function to reset the workout service to its initial state. It frees any memory associated with the current workout and clears all workout data. Call this function when you need to discard the current workout session and start fresh. Ensure that no other operations are being performed on the workout data when calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`workout_service_reset`](<../../../../src/fw/services/normal/activity/workout_service.c.md#workout_service_reset>)  (Implementation)
